vcha.name: vCenter High Availability (vCHA)

notices.title: vCenter High Availability
bullets: vCenter High Availability (vCHA) is not supported for Convert or Import

steps: Disable vCenter High Availability(vCHA) - All Guidance is provided in link ((View Documentation)

elm_upgrade_ring.name: Enhanced Linked Mode (ELM) - Upgrade All vCenters

notices.title: ELM Upgrade Requirements
bullets: ALL vCenter instances in the ELM ring must be upgraded to maintain ELM
Upgrade vCenters sequentially, one at a time
Verify replication status after each vCenter upgrade

steps: Break ELM After All vCenters are upgrade to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link ((View Documentation)

elm_break.name: Enhanced Linked Mode (ELM) - Break Configuration

notices.title: Breaking Enhanced Linked Mode
bullets: Breaking ELM is REQUIRED for VCF conversion
Breaking ELM is REQUIRED when using NSX in vSphere 8
This action cannot be easily reversed
Document the configuration before breaking

steps: Break ELM on 8.0 vCenters that has NSX - All Guidance is provided in link ((View Documentation)

vcenter.name: vCenter
steps: Upgrade vCenter to version 9.1 using standard vSphere upgrade process - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Integrated Windows Authentication on vCenter
bullets: Replace Integrated Windows Authentication configured with another IDP configuration

notices.title: vCenter Location
bullets: Virtual machine of vCenter instance hosted on one of the clusters that is managed by the same instance

notices.title: VXRAIL
bullets: vCenters or Clusters managed by Dell VxRail are not supported in VCF Upgrade today for vSphere environments

esx.name: ESX
steps: All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Upgrade Virtual Distributed Switch - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
(Optional) Upgrade vSAN On-Disk Format Versions - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: ESX bootbank
bullets: Validate ESX bootbank is at least 1gb in size by running command on host df -h /bootbank

notices.title: Check HCL for Hardware support on VCF 9.1
bullets: Intel Skylake (Broadwell, Kabylake & Skylake) Processors no longer supported (deprecated in earlier releases), requires RPQ

vum_migration.name: VUM-Based Clusters
steps: Migrate from vLCM Baselines to vLCM Images - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: vSphere Lifecycle Management with Baselines is no longer supported
bullets: vSphere Lifecycle Management with Baselines is no longer supported, clusters will need to be transition as part of the upgrade which is discussed on the upgrade paths

operations.name: VCF Operations
steps: Upgrade VCF Operations to version 9.1 compatible release - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Upgrade Options when having multiple VCF Operations
bullets: Multiple VCF Fleets
Consolidate to one Instance
Upgrade Operations as a Monitoring Only Instance

convert_with_vsphere_only.name: Convert to VCF 9.1

steps: Deploy of VCF Installer - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Downloading Binaries to VCF Installer - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Start convert to VCF - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Conversion Process
bullets: This step converts your vSphere environment to VCF 9.1
SDDC Manager will manage lifecycle operations after conversion
If VCF Operations was not previously installed, it will be deployed during this step as it is required for VCF

notices.title: Automation Deployment
bullets: VCF Automation will be deployed in a subsequent phase after VCF conversion

automation_install_notices.title: New VCF Automation Installation
bullets: VCF Automation is being installed fresh as part of this VCF conversion
Includes Automation Orchestrator for workflow automation
Configure cloud zones and endpoints post-deployment
Set up blueprints and catalogs after deployment is complete

automation_install_notices.title: Resource Requirements
bullets: Ensure sufficient resources are available for all Automation components
Review minimum requirements in documentation before proceeding

logs.name: Log Management
steps: Deploy new Log Management 9.1 services - there is no in-place upgrade path. All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Multiple Logs are not supported
bullets: There is an option to migrate 90 days of logs from VCF Operation for Logs 8.18.x instance

network.name: VCF Operations for Network
steps: Upgrade VCF Operations for Network to version 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

replication.name: vSphere Replication
steps: Upgrade vSphere Replication to version 9.1 - ll Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

srm.name: Site Recovery Manager
steps: Upgrade Site Recovery Manager to version 9.1 - ll Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

avi.name: AVI (NSX ALB)
steps: Upgrade NSX Advanced Load Balancer to compatible version - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

hcx.name: HCX
steps: Upgrade HCX to version compatible with vSphere 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vks.name: VKS (vSphere Kubernetes Supervisor Service)
steps: Upgrade vSphere with Tanzu to version 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VKS deployed on vSphere Lifecycle Baseline Clusters(VUM)
bullets: Customers with VKS clusters deployed on vSphere Lifecycle Baseline Clusters are not supported, a migration to vSphere Lifecycle Images(vLCM) is required - see documentation

vsan_witness.name: vSAN Witness Host
steps: Upgrade vSAN Witness host to version 9.1 - will happen as part of ESX upgrade after Convert

notices.title: vSAN Witness Host Upgrade
bullets: vSAN Witness hosts must be upgraded before ESX hosts in stretched clusters

nsx.name: NSX
steps: Upgrade NSX Local Managers to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: NSX Version Compatibility
bullets: Ensure NSX version is compatible with vSphere 9.1
Review VMware Product Interoperability Matrix

nsx_federation.name: NSX Federation
steps: Upgrade NSX Global Managers to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Federation Prerequisites
bullets: All NSX sites must be on compatible versions
Network connectivity required between all sites
Upgrade BEFORE NSX Local Managers

operations_prereq.name: Pre-Upgrade: Upgrade VCF Operations to 8.18.x

notices.title: Required Before Proceeding
bullets: Your current VCF Operations version must be upgraded to 8.18.x before upgrading to 9.1

steps: Upgrade VCF Operations Standalone - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Upgrade VCF Operations managed by vRSLCM - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

nsx_prereq.name: Pre-Upgrade: Upgrade NSX to 4.2.x

notices.title: Required Before Proceeding
bullets: NSX 4.1.x must be upgraded to 4.2.x before proceeding with the VCF upgrade path

steps: NSX upgrade - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

automation_prereq.name: Pre-Upgrade: Upgrade VCF Automation to 8.18.x

notices.title: Required Before Proceeding
bullets: Your current VCF Automation version must be upgraded to 8.18.x before upgrading to 9.1

steps: Upgrade VCF Automation - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vcenter_esx_prereq.name: Pre-Upgrade: Upgrade vCenter and ESX to 8.0u3

notices.title: Required Before Proceeding
bullets: Your current vCenter and/or ESX version must be upgraded to 8.0u3 before continuing

steps: vCenter upgrade - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
ESX upgrade - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
